methods.description: Statistical procedures used to analyze microbial data, including transformations, model selection, and redundancy analysis to understand variance and clustering.

steps.description: Log-transform with zero-replacement all columns on all levels to resolve the closed composition of the microbial data and remove any forced correlations.
steps.searchable_summary: apply log transformation with zero-replacement to microbial data

steps.description: Subject context data to column-wise Hellinger transformation to ameliorate variability.
steps.searchable_summary: perform Hellinger transformation on context data

steps.description: Perform PCA on phylum, class, and order levels.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct PCA on microbial data at various taxonomic levels
inputs: microbial data at phylum, class, order levels

steps.description: Use the iterative ordistep function in the R package vegan to determine the subset of context variables that best explain the variance expressed by the microbial community.
steps.searchable_summary: select context variables using ordistep function in R

steps.description: Run the ordistep function 100 times for each core and taxonomic level to ensure robust model selection.
steps.searchable_summary: validate model selection by running ordistep 100 times

steps.description: Control selected models for variance inflation using the vif.cca function in vegan.
steps.searchable_summary: check for variance inflation using vif.cca in R

steps.description: Perform Redundancy Analysis (RDA) on the microbial data using the selected minimum adequate model variables as constraints.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct RDA using selected model variables

steps.description: Use Hierarchical Cluster Analysis with Euclidean distance and the square root of Ward's agglomeration criterion to assess sample clustering.
Perform analysis of similarities between groups of samples using the anosim function with 9999 permutations in the R package vegan.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze similarities using anosim in R

steps.description: Correlate primary and secondary eigenvectors of PCA and RDA using Spearman's rho to quantify compliance between analyses.
steps.searchable_summary: correlate PCA and RDA eigenvectors using Spearman's rho

steps.description: Correlate variance in the unconstrained microbial community structure with variance in selected geochemical variables.
steps.searchable_summary: correlate microbial community variance with geochemical variance

methods.description: Procedures for sampling microbial communities, extracting DNA, and processing sequences for analysis.

steps.description: Subsample cores at regular depth intervals using sterile cut-off syringes.
Extract genomic DNA from approximately 0.5 g of sediments using the FastDNA R spin kit for soil.
steps.searchable_summary: extract DNA from sediments using FastDNA kit

steps.description: Prepare 16S rRNA gene amplicon libraries by PCR amplification using specific primers.
Pool all libraries in a 1:1 ratio based on DNA concentration and sequence using an Ion Torrent Personal Genome Machine.
steps.searchable_summary: pool and sequence libraries using Ion Torrent

steps.description: Filter and cluster reads denovo at 97% similarity into OTUs using USEARCH and UPARSE algorithms.
steps.searchable_summary: cluster reads into OTUs using USEARCH and UPARSE

steps.description: Trim sequences to 220 base pairs and perform taxonomic classification using CREST with the SilvaMod reference database.
steps.searchable_summary: classify OTUs using CREST and SilvaMod

steps.description: Discard OTUs based on contamination control criteria using blank samples.
Remove singletons and subsample to 15058 reads.
